title: Events, Refs, SHAs, Forks, and Workflow Trust Boundaries · Independent Delivery Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar repository, event, runner, and trust constraint and must transfer events, refs, shas, forks, and workflow trust boundaries into a new event and revision trust policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of event and revision trust policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive workflow source, checked-out revision, GITHUB_REF, GITHUB_SHA, actor, permissions, and payload from each trigger.
Choose pull request merge, head, or base revision intentionally and state what integration or source claim each can prove.
Model fork approvals, withheld secrets, read-only tokens, compute abuse, contributor changes, and re-run authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Event Payload Ref Sha
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab maintainer runs fork code through pull_request_target with write authority, then a privileged workflow_run consumes its artifact. Establish workflow source, tested revision, actor, permissions, producer trust, and data-only inspection. Competency probe: Derive workflow source, checked-out revision, GITHUB_REF, GITHUB_SHA, actor, permissions, and payload from each trigger.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-workflow-run-artifact-trust-8jty03". Tailor its event-trust-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, keep Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-workflow-run-artifact-trust-8jty03
  - competency: ci-workflow-run-artifact-trust
    artifact: event-trust-policy.yaml
    decision: workflow run artifact trust
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: remove one supported Node and operating-system matrix cell
    retained-proof: OIDC claims provider condition service account IAM and audit entry
    case-key: 8jty03
    change: |
      event: pull_request
      workflow-source: merge-commit
      checkout-revision: pull-request-merge-sha
      token: read-only
      secrets: withheld
      untrusted-code: isolated-execution
      privileged-followup: data-only
      producer-proof: run-id-and-workflow-sha
    verify: compare pull_request pull_request_target workflow_run refs SHAs tokens secrets and checked-out code
    failure: Reject fork code under pull_request_target secrets, maintainer rerun as trust, or successful artifacts as executable proof.
    repair: Use low-privilege pull_request execution, separate privileged follow-up, and validate producer and content strictly as data.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab maintainer runs fork code through pull_request_target with write authority, then a privileged workflow_run consumes its artifact. Establish workflow source, tested revision, actor, permissions, producer trust, and data-only inspection. Competency probe: Treat workflow_run, issue_comment, repository_dispatch, and downloaded artifacts according to producer identity, revision, permissions, and content trust.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: An artifact from a successful low-privilege workflow is safe to execute in a privileged workflow.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab maintainer runs fork code through pull_request_target with write authority, then a privileged workflow_run consumes its artifact. Establish workflow source, tested revision, actor, permissions, producer trust, and data-only inspection. Competency probe: Treat workflow_run, issue_comment, repository_dispatch, and downloaded artifacts according to producer identity, revision, permissions, and content trust.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, keep Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat workflow_run, issue_comment, repository_dispatch, and downloaded artifacts according to producer identity, revision, permissions, and content trust.
